evals.prompt: I'm working in acme-website (a Level 2 client project based on project-directus-nextjs) and I just realized that the .env.example in the parent template is missing VERCEL_TOKEN and VERCEL_ORG_ID. Every Directus+Next.js project needs these for deployment. Can you push this fix up to the Level 1 template?
evals.expected_output: The feedback skill reads stack.json to find the parent (project-directus-nextjs), locates it at $PROJECT_TEMPLATES_DIR, adds VERCEL_TOKEN and VERCEL_ORG_ID to its .env.example under a Vercel section, records the change in LEARNINGS.md with source attribution to acme-website, and commits.
expectations: Reads stack.json from the current project to determine parent template name
Identifies the target as Level 1 (project-directus-nextjs), not Level 0
Locates the parent template directory using $PROJECT_TEMPLATES_DIR
Adds VERCEL_TOKEN and VERCEL_ORG_ID to the parent's .env.example
Appends an entry to the parent's LEARNINGS.md with the source project name (acme-website)
Creates a git commit in the parent template repo with a descriptive message
Notes which child projects should merge from the updated parent

evals.prompt: We keep running into the same issue across all our projects — people accidentally run git push --force to main and it wrecks everyone's branches. This isn't specific to any stack, it should be a universal safety rule in the base template. Add it to project-template's safety rules.
evals.expected_output: The feedback skill identifies this as a Level 0 (universal) improvement. It navigates to project-template (not a Level 1 template), creates or updates .claude/rules/safety.md with a rule about never force-pushing to main, records in LEARNINGS.md, and commits.
expectations: Identifies the target as Level 0 (project-template) since this is universal across all stacks
Does NOT target a Level 1 stack-specific template
Locates project-template directory at $PROJECT_TEMPLATES_DIR/project-template
Creates or updates .claude/rules/safety.md with a force-push prevention rule
The rule includes a WHY explanation (not just a bare prohibition)
Records in project-template's LEARNINGS.md
Commits to the project-template repo

evals.prompt: I just spent an hour figuring out that shadcn v4 components use @base-ui/react instead of Radix, and the asChild prop doesn't exist anymore — you have to use render instead. This gotcha should definitely be in the Level 1 template's CLAUDE.md so nobody else wastes time on it.
evals.expected_output: The feedback skill identifies this as stack-specific (Level 1, project-directus-nextjs). It locates the parent template, adds the shadcn v4 gotcha to the CLAUDE.md Gotchas section, records in LEARNINGS.md, and commits.
expectations: Identifies the target as Level 1 (stack-specific) since shadcn/base-ui is Next.js-specific
Reads stack.json to resolve the Level 1 parent template
Adds the gotcha about shadcn v4 and @base-ui/react to CLAUDE.md's Gotchas section
The gotcha mentions both the old pattern (asChild) and the new pattern (render)
Records in the parent template's LEARNINGS.md
Commits with a descriptive message referencing the gotcha

evals.prompt: I need to add a new skill to the project-template base. It should be called 'code-review-checklist' and guide developers through a structured code review process before creating PRs. This is useful for every project regardless of stack.
evals.expected_output: The feedback skill identifies this as a Level 0 improvement (universal skill), locates project-template, creates .claude/skills/code-review-checklist/SKILL.md with proper frontmatter and structured code review steps, records in LEARNINGS.md, and commits.
expectations: Identifies the target as Level 0 (project-template) since code review is universal
Creates .claude/skills/code-review-checklist/SKILL.md in the parent template
The SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter with name and description fields
The skill body contains structured code review steps in imperative form
Records the addition in project-template's LEARNINGS.md
Commits with a feat() message since this is a new skill

evals.prompt: I'm in a Level 2 project (acme-website, parent is project-directus-nextjs) and I want to add a 'new-api-route' skill that scaffolds Next.js API routes with Directus data fetching. This is specific to the Directus+Next.js stack. Push it to the Level 1 template.
evals.expected_output: The feedback skill routes this to Level 1 (project-directus-nextjs), creates the new-api-route skill with proper SKILL.md frontmatter and Next.js + Directus-specific content, records in LEARNINGS.md, and commits.
expectations: Routes to Level 1 (project-directus-nextjs), not Level 0, since this is stack-specific
Reads stack.json from acme-website to resolve the parent
Creates .claude/skills/new-api-route/SKILL.md in project-directus-nextjs
The skill's SKILL.md includes Next.js API route patterns with Directus SDK usage
Has proper YAML frontmatter with name and description including trigger phrases
Records in project-directus-nextjs's LEARNINGS.md with source attribution
Uses feat() commit convention since this is a new skill addition
